name: hig-project-context
description: Create or update a shared Apple design context document that other HIG skills use to tailor guidance. Use when the user says 'set up my project context,' 'what platforms am I targeting,' 'configure HIG settings,' or when starting a new Apple platform project.
capabilities: Auto-discover project context from README.md, Package.swift, Info.plist, and existing code
Generate apple-design-context.md with product, platform, and technology information
Update existing context documents while preserving unchanged information
Coordinate with other HIG skills to provide consistent, targeted guidance
Analyze accessibility implementation through import statement detection
Support all Apple platforms: iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, visionOS
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Prompt-only skill with no executable code. The skill creates a markdown context file for Apple Human Interface Guidelines workflows. No security risks detected. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code. Risk score: 0/100.
valueStatement: Stop answering repetitive platform questions. This skill creates a shared design context file that all HIG skills reference automatically.
limitations: Only reads files from the current project directory
Cannot detect design decisions not present in code or configuration files
Requires user confirmation for auto-discovered context information
Context document must be manually updated when project requirements change

useCases.title: New iOS Project Setup
useCases.description: Set up design context when starting a new iOS or iPadOS app to ensure all HIG skills provide platform-specific guidance from the start.

useCases.title: Multi-Platform App Configuration
useCases.description: Configure design context for apps targeting multiple Apple platforms to receive consistent guidance across iOS, macOS, and other platforms.

useCases.title: Design System Documentation
useCases.description: Document existing design system choices and accessibility requirements so HIG skills can provide tailored recommendations that match established patterns.

promptTemplates.title: Initial Project Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Set up my Apple design project context. I'm building a new iOS app.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when starting a new Apple platform project and want to configure design context for the first time.

promptTemplates.title: Update Existing Context
promptTemplates.prompt: Update my Apple design context. We've added macOS support and changed our accessibility requirements.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when project requirements have changed and the existing context document needs updating.

promptTemplates.title: Review Current Context
promptTemplates.prompt: Review my current Apple design context and let me know if any information is missing or outdated.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use to check if the existing context document is complete and accurate.

promptTemplates.title: Multi-Platform Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Configure my Apple design context for a universal app that runs on iOS, iPadOS, and macOS with SwiftUI.
promptTemplates.scenario: Use when setting up context for apps targeting multiple Apple platforms simultaneously.

bestPractices: Run this skill before using other HIG skills to ensure they have complete project context
Keep the context document updated when platform targets or design requirements change
Review auto-discovered information for accuracy before confirming the context document
Store the context file at .claude/apple-design-context.md so other skills can find it automatically
antiPatterns: Do not manually edit the context file while other HIG skills are actively using it
Do not skip the user confirmation step when auto-discovering project information
Do not create multiple context files in different locations
Do not use this skill for non-Apple platform projects

faq.question: Where is the context document stored?
faq.answer: The context document is created at .claude/apple-design-context.md in your project root directory. All HIG skills check this location automatically.

faq.question: What if I do not have a Package.swift or Info.plist file?
faq.answer: The skill will still work. It auto-discovers information from available files like README.md and existing code, then asks you for any missing details.

faq.question: Can I edit the context document manually?
faq.answer: Yes, but it is recommended to use this skill to ensure the format stays consistent with what other HIG skills expect. Manual edits may be overwritten.

faq.question: How does this skill coordinate with other HIG skills?
faq.answer: Other HIG skills like hig-platforms and hig-foundations check for the context document first. If found, they use that information instead of asking repetitive questions.

faq.question: What platforms does this skill support?
faq.answer: All Apple platforms are supported: iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, and visionOS. The skill helps you specify which platforms your app targets.

faq.question: Do I need to run this skill every time I use a HIG skill?
faq.answer: No, run it once when setting up your project or when requirements change. The context document persists and other HIG skills will reference it automatically.
